bull.b: A completed $11.5M offering and pro forma cash of ~$12.4M materially extends runway into 2027, reducing near-term refinancing pressure and enabling completion of pivotal pilots, prototype commercialization and AI development. This gives management time to de-risk regulatory and commercial milestones before the next fun

Pivoting to a licensing-led model lowers capital intensity and operating costs, leverages partners’ distribution scale, and accelerates global adoption. A lean implementation team and founder-as-operator can focus on IP, algorithms and partner integrations, improving capital efficiency and long-term scalability of reve

Existing FDA clearances plus active pilots (ALIGN‑ACS enrollment ahead of schedule and patch pilot initiation) materially advance regulatory and clinical validation. These durable milestones underpin potential label expansion, enable AI training on real-world data, and strengthen commercial conversations with health sy

bear.b: A prolonged pre‑revenue profile with annual net losses near $13–$21M and ~ $20M TTM means the business must continue funding operations until commercial scale. Persistent cash losses impede internal reinvestment, heighten dilution risk from future financings, and lengthen the timeline to sustainable, self-funded growth

A collapsing equity base to roughly $0.6M TTM removes a margin of safety and raises solvency concerns if cash consumption persists. Even with no debt, low shareholder equity constrains strategic optionality, makes future capital raises more dilutive, and reduces attractiveness to partners or acquirers evaluating balanc

A formal Nasdaq minimum bid‑price deficiency is a structural governance and liquidity risk: potential delisting would impair trading liquidity, complicate capital raises and could deter commercial partners.
